steps.explanation: Compute the mean of data set A: 1 + 2 + 3 + 4 + 5 + 6 + 7 = 28. There are 7 values, so mean A = 28 ÷ 7 = 4.
Compute the mean of data set B: 1 + 1 + 2 + 2 + 3 + 3 + 4 = 16. There are 7 values, so mean B = 16 ÷ 7 ≈ 2.29.
Compare: mean A = 4 > mean B ≈ 2.29. The mean of data set A is greater than the mean of data set B.

Evaluate all choices:
  • A: Both means equal 2. Mean A = 4 and mean B ≈ 2.29; neither equals 2. Eliminated.
  • B: Both means equal 4. Mean B ≈ 2.29, not 4. Eliminated.
  • C: Mean A < mean B. Mean A = 4 > mean B ≈ 2.29, so this is backwards. Eliminated.
  • D: Mean A > mean B. Mean A = 4 and mean B ≈ 2.29, so A's mean is greater. That matches choice D — D is correct.
